refined_fr.refined_fr: The NGHE solution shall update the worksite model within the customer-required timeliness for operational use by minimizing data acquisition latency and processing latency.
refined_fr.rationale: To meet the overall latency requirement, the update process is decomposed into two key sub-functions: acquiring timely data from the worksite and processing that data efficiently to update the model.
refined_fr.verification_idea: Measure and verify the latency of data acquisition and processing separately, then combined, ensuring the p95 latency is ≤ 1.0 s under supported update scenarios.
sub_frs: FR1: Acquire worksite data with minimal latency
FR2: Process and update worksite model with minimal latency

candidate_dps: DP1: Use high-frequency telemetry data streams with priority network scheduling
DP2: Implement edge computing for local data pre-processing
DP3: Optimize update algorithms for incremental model updates
selected_dp.selected_dp: DP1: Use high-frequency telemetry data streams with priority network scheduling

potential_sources: Telemetry data dependency on network link quality
Processing algorithms coupling with data acquisition timing
coupling_check.notes: The selected DP logically separates data acquisition (high-frequency telemetry) from processing, minimizing coupling. Priority network scheduling reduces latency variability.

assumptions_risks: Assumes network link supports priority scheduling and sufficient bandwidth
Risk of network congestion impacting telemetry timeliness
Assumes telemetry data format is compatible with processing modules
selected_dp.recommended_revision: Consider adding edge computing (DP2) if network conditions degrade or latency targets are not met.

im_boundary.name: NGHE Solution
im_boundary.scope: Overall system responsible for telemetry data acquisition, processing, and worksite model update

external_interfaces.name: Telemetry
external_interfaces.details: Receives high-frequency telemetry data streams from Vehicle and Teleoperation modules

external_interfaces.name: Network Link
external_interfaces.details: Supports priority scheduling for telemetry data transmission

module_containment_scores.notes: Centralizes telemetry data acquisition and processing, minimizing diffusion and maximizing containment.
Primarily data source; limited containment of update logic.
Supports telemetry but not responsible for update processing.
Focused on safety alerts, unrelated to update latency.

assigned_im.containment_hypothesis: Centralizing the update latency functionality within the NGHE Solution module reduces interface complexity and improves latency performance.
containment_risks: Potential bottleneck if NGHE Solution module becomes overloaded
Dependency on network link quality may still impact latency
assigned_im.recommended_revision: Monitor performance and consider distributing processing to Vehicle or Teleoperation modules if latency targets are not met.

fr_dp_gate.reason: The refined FRs are logically decomposed and the selected DP addresses the key latency requirements with clear assumptions and manageable coupling.

diffusion_risks: Potential over-centralization in NGHE Solution module
Network link dependency may cause latency spikes
dp_im_gate.containment_notes: Containment scores favor NGHE Solution as the best architectural boundary for this DP.

fr_im_gate.verification_feasibility: Verification can be performed by measuring telemetry latency and update processing times within the NGHE Solution module using existing telemetry and network interfaces.
fr_im_gate.allocation_clarity: Clear allocation of FRs to NGHE Solution with well-defined interfaces to Vehicle and Teleoperation.

recommended_revisions: Add contingency plans for network degradation scenarios
Consider edge computing augmentation if latency targets are not consistently met

decision.justification: The current level of decomposition, DP selection, and IM assignment satisfy the latency requirement with clear architectural boundaries and manageable risks. Verification approaches are feasible.
next_level_sub_frs: FR1: Acquire worksite data with minimal latency
FR2: Process and update worksite model with minimal latency

revise.why: No immediate revisions required; monitor performance and revisit if risks materialize.

assumptions: Telemetry data format and frequency are sufficient for latency targets
open_risks: Potential processing bottlenecks in NGHE Solution module
evidence_needed: Latency measurements of telemetry data acquisition and processing
Network performance statistics under operational conditions
